description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the registry.gitlab.com/itxtech/docker-env-cenisys Docker image and three build-stage jobs: build:gcc runs in the build stage, creates a build directory, runs cmake with CC=gcc CXX=g++ and installs to usr/ via DESTDIR=.. ninja install, caches the build/ directory (untracked) and stores the usr/ directory as an artifact named cenisys-gcc-${CI_BUILD_REF:0:7}; build:unit-test runs in the build stage, creates a build directory, runs cmake with clang, enables profiling flags, installs to prefix/, caches build/, and archives the prefix/ directory as cenisys-test-${CI_BUILD_REF:0:7}; clang-static runs in the build stage, creates a build directory, runs scan-build with cmake in Debug mode and then scan-build ninja, and saves the report/ directory as cenisys-clang-report-${CI_BUILD_REF:0:7}. Add a test:unit-test job in the test stage that depends on build:unit-test, executes the built test binary with LLVM_PROFILE_FILE, merges profiling data with llvm-profdata, generates a coverage report with llvm-cov, and publishes unit-test.profdata as an artifact named cenisys-test-cov-${CI_BUILD_REF:0:7}. Finally, add a pages job in the deploy stage that depends on build:gcc, copies the generated documentation from usr/share/doc/cenisys/html/ to a public/ folder, publishes the public/ directory as a Pages artifact, and runs only on the master branch.
